# @mongez/collection â€” full reference

> A chainable, immutable-by-default array wrapper. This is the concatenated reference; load `llms.txt` for the structured index.

## Install

```sh
yarn add @mongez/collection
# deps: @mongez/reinforcements, @mongez/supportive-is
```

## Public exports

```ts
import {
  collect,
  ImmutableCollection,
  Operators,
  type ComparisonOperator,
  type GenericObject,
} from "@mongez/collection";
```

## collect / ImmutableCollection

> **Auto-trigger:** code imports `collect`, `ImmutableCollection`, `collect.create`, `collect.fromIterator`, or `ImmutableCollection.fromIterator` / `ImmutableCollection.create` from `@mongez/collection`; user asks "how do I create a collection", "how to wrap an array / Set / Map / generator", "how to seed N items", "how to make a collection from an iterable"; `import { collect, ImmutableCollection } from "@mongez/collection"`.
> **Skip when:** operating on an already-built collection (filter / map / sort / aggregate) â€” use the operation-specific skills (`mongez-collection-querying`, `mongez-collection-transforming`, etc.); `@mongez/reinforcements` standalone helpers â€” use `mongez-reinforcements-arrays` for one-shot helpers without a wrapper.

```ts
collect<T>(items?: T[] | ImmutableCollection<T>): ImmutableCollection<T>

new ImmutableCollection<T>(items?: T[] | ImmutableCollection<T>)

ImmutableCollection.fromIterator<T>(iterable: Iterable<any>): ImmutableCollection<T>
ImmutableCollection.create<T>(length: number, initialValue?: T | ((i: number) => T)): ImmutableCollection<T>

collect.fromIterator = ImmutableCollection.fromIterator
collect.create       = ImmutableCollection.create
```

The constructor clones the input array. Passing anything other than an array or another collection throws `Invalid items type`.

## Mutation reference (read this first)

> **Auto-trigger:** code calls `c.sort`, `c.reverse`, `c.flip`, `c.sortByDesc`, `c.shift`, `c.pop`, `c.clone`, or `c.copy` on an `ImmutableCollection`; user asks "is sort/reverse/shift/pop safe", "why is my original collection changing", "do I need to clone before sorting", "which methods mutate in @mongez/collection", "is toArray a copy"; debugging an unexpected mutation bug on a shared collection.
> **Skip when:** non-mutating sort by key (`sortBy(key)` and `sortBy({...})`) â€” use `mongez-collection-sort-group`; insert / remove / replace operations that always return new â€” use `mongez-collection-overview` for the global picture; understanding what `@mongez/reinforcements` does on its own â€” that package has no wrapper to mutate.

Despite the "Immutable" prefix, four methods mutate the underlying array:

| Method | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `sort(cb?)` | Calls `Array.prototype.sort` on `this.items` directly. Source is reordered. |
| `reverse()` / `flip()` | Calls `Array.prototype.reverse` on `this.items`. Source is reversed. |
| `sortByDesc(key)` | Comparator-based but operates on `this.items` (no clone). |
| `shift()` | Returns the removed first item; mutates source. |
| `pop()` | Returns the removed last item; mutates source. |

Workaround: `c.clone().sort(...)` or `c.clone().reverse()`.

Everything else returns a new collection.

## Array-builtin parity

> **Auto-trigger:** code calls any of `c.map`, `c.filter`, `c.flat`, `c.flatMap`, `c.reduce`, `c.reduceRight`, `c.find`, `c.findIndex`, `c.indexOf`, `c.lastIndexOf`, `c.includes`, `c.contains`, `c.every`, `c.some`, `c.join`, `c.implode`, `c.forEach`, `c.each`, `c.keys`, `c.values`, `c.entries`, `c.indexes`, `c.toArray`, `c.all`, `c.toJson`, `c.toString`, `c.takeWhile`, `c.removeAll` on an `ImmutableCollection`; user asks "how do I map / filter / reduce a collection", "why is reduce returning NaN", "how do I unwrap to a plain array", "is toArray a copy"; file iterates / spreads / `Array.from`s a collection.
> **Skip when:** operator-based filtering (`where(...)`) â€” use `mongez-collection-where` or `mongez-collection-querying`; aggregate math (`sum`/`avg`/`min`/`max`) â€” use `mongez-collection-math`; `pluck` / `select` / `groupBy` / `partition` â€” use `mongez-collection-transforming` or `mongez-collection-sort-group`; standalone array helpers without `@mongez/collection` â€” use `mongez-reinforcements-arrays` instead.

```ts
// Transforms (return new collection)
c.map<U>(cb): ImmutableCollection<U>
c.filter(cb): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.takeWhile(cb): ImmutableCollection<T>          // alias for filter (kept-items)
c.removeAll(cb): ImmutableCollection<T>          // alias for filter (kept-items; confusing name)
c.flat(depth?: number): ImmutableCollection<any>
c.flatMap(cb): ImmutableCollection<any>

// Reads (return scalar)
c.reduce<Acc>(cb, initialValue?): Acc
c.reduceRight(cb, initialValue?): any
c.find(cb): T | undefined
c.findIndex(cb): number
c.indexOf(item, fromIndex?): number
c.lastIndexOf(item, fromIndex?): number
c.includes(item): boolean
c.contains(item): boolean                        // alias
c.has(cb): boolean                               // findIndex !== -1
c.every(cb): boolean
c.some(cb): boolean
c.join(separator?): string
c.implode(separator?): string                    // alias

// Iteration / shape
c.forEach(cb): this
c.each(cb): this                                 // alias
c.tap(cb: (c) => any): this                      // side-effect, returns self
c.keys(): ImmutableCollection<number>
c.values(): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.entries(): ImmutableCollection<[number, T]>
c.indexes(): ImmutableCollection<number>         // same as keys
c.length: number                                 // getter (not a method)
c[Symbol.iterator](): Iterator<T>

// Conversion
c.toArray(map?): T[]                             // returns live reference
c.all(): T[]                                     // alias for toArray()
c.toString(): string
c.toJson(): string
```

> **Known issue**: `c.reduce(cb)` (no initialValue) passes `undefined` through to `Array.prototype.reduce` instead of omitting the arg, producing `NaN` for numeric arrays. Always provide an initial value.

## Insert / remove

```ts
// Insert (returns new)
c.push(...items): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.append(...items): ImmutableCollection<T>       // alias
c.pushUnique(...items): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.unshift(...items): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.prepend(...items): ImmutableCollection<T>      // alias for unshift
c.prependUnique(...items): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.unshiftUnique(...items): ImmutableCollection<T> // alias for prependUnique

// Quirk: `prependUnique(a, b, c)` unshifts items one-by-one, so the
// final ordering is the REVERSE of the argument list (each new item
// goes to position 0).

// Remove (returns new)
c.delete(index): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.unset(...indexes): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.remove(value): ImmutableCollection<T>          // drops first strict-equal match; no-op if not found

// Remove (MUTATES + returns the removed item)
c.shift(): T | undefined
c.pop(): T | undefined

// Replace (returns new)
c.set(index, value): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.update(index, value): ImmutableCollection<T>   // alias for set
c.replace(oldValue, newValue): ImmutableCollection<T>     // first occurrence
c.replaceAll(oldValue, newValue): ImmutableCollection<T>  // every occurrence

// Reorder (returns new)
c.swap(i, j): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.move(from, to): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.reorder({ [oldIndex]: newIndex }): ImmutableCollection<T>
```

## Merge

```ts
c.merge(...arrays): ImmutableCollection<T>       // concat with given arrays
c.concat(...arrays): ImmutableCollection<T>      // alias
```

## Reads

```ts
c.first(): T | undefined
c.last(): T | undefined
c.end(): T | undefined                           // alias for last

c.at(i): T | undefined
c.index(i): T | undefined                        // alias for at

c.get(path): any                                 // dot-notation read on items
c.value(key, default?): any                      // first item's value for that key
c.valueAt(index, key, default?): any             // one item's value for a key
c.lastValue(key, default?): any                  // last item's value for that key

c.getByIndexes(...indexes): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.exceptIndexes(...indexes): ImmutableCollection<T>

c.equals(other: T[] | ImmutableCollection<T>): boolean   // deep equality
c.isEmpty(): boolean
c.isNotEmpty(): boolean
c.lastIndex(): number                            // length - 1
```

## where(...) â€” operator filtering

> **Auto-trigger:** code calls `c.where`, `c.whereIn`, `c.whereNot`, `c.whereBetween`, `c.whereNotBetween`, `c.whereEmpty`, `c.whereNotEmpty`, `c.heavy`, `c.whereNull`, `c.whereNotNull`, `c.whereUndefined`, `c.whereNotUndefined`, `c.whereExists`, `c.whereNotExists`, `c.firstWhere`, `c.lastWhere`, or imports `Operators` / `ComparisonOperator` from `@mongez/collection`; user asks "how do I filter by `>` / `like` / `between` / `in` / `null`", "what operators does where support", "how to use a RegExp with where", "is `where(key, 'is undefined')` broken".
> **Skip when:** predicate-callback filtering (`filter`, `reject`, `except`, `not`) â€” use `mongez-collection-querying` or `mongez-collection-builtins`; aggregations or projections downstream of a filter â€” chain into `mongez-collection-math` or `mongez-collection-transforming`; `@mongez/reinforcements` has NO `where(...)` operator engine â€” recommend this skill over `mongez-reinforcements-arrays` when the user needs operator filtering.

Three overloads:

```ts
c.where(key, value): ImmutableCollection<T>                       // equality
c.where(key, operator, value): ImmutableCollection<T>             // any operator
c.where(operator, value): ImmutableCollection<T>                  // primitive-mode (currently broken)
```

Operators (50+):

- Equality: `=` / `equals`, `!=` / `not` / `not equals`
- Comparison: `>` / `gt`, `>=` / `gte`, `<` / `lt`, `<=` / `lte`
- Substring (case-insensitive): `like` / `%`, `not like` / `!%`
- Regex: `regex` (or pass a `RegExp` value with no operator)
- Set membership: `in`, `not in` / `!in`
- Range (inclusive): `between` / `<>`, `not between` / `!between` / `!<>`
- Substring (`.includes`): `contains`, `not contains` / `!contains`
- Boundary: `starts with`, `not starts with` / `!starts with`, `ends with`, `not ends with` / `!ends with`
- Null: `null` / `is null`, `is not null` / `!null`
- Undefined: `undefined` / `is undefined`, `is not undefined` / `!undefined`
- Existence: `exists`, `not exists` / `!exists`
- Boolean: `true` / `is true`, `is not true` / `!true`, `false` / `is false`, `is not false` / `!false`
- typeof: `is` / `typeof`, `is not` / `!is` / `not typeof`
- instanceof: `instanceof` / `is a`, `not instanceof` / `!instanceof` / `is not a` / `!is a`
- Empty: `empty` / `is empty`, `not empty` / `is not empty` / `!empty`

`firstWhere(...)`, `lastWhere(...)` are `c.where(...).first()` / `c.where(...).last()` shortcuts.

Dedicated shorthands:

```ts
c.whereIn(values | key, values): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.whereNot(value | key, value): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.whereBetween(values | key, values): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.whereNotBetween(values | key, values): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.whereEmpty(key?): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.whereNotEmpty(key?): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.heavy(key?): ImmutableCollection<T>                            // alias for whereNotEmpty
c.whereNull(key?): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.whereNotNull(key?): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.whereUndefined(key?): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.whereNotUndefined(key?): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.whereExists(key): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.whereNotExists(key): ImmutableCollection<T>
```

Inverse:

```ts
c.reject(cb): ImmutableCollection<T>             // !cb
c.except(cb): ImmutableCollection<T>             // alias for reject
c.skipWhile(cb): ImmutableCollection<T>          // alias for reject
c.not(value): ImmutableCollection<T>             // !== value
c.rejectFirst(cb) / exceptFirst(cb): ImmutableCollection<T>   // drop first match only
c.rejectLast(cb) / exceptLast(cb): ImmutableCollection<T>     // drop last match only
```

### Known where(...) bugs

- `where(operator, value)` primitive mode doesn't rotate args; treat `(">", 2)` as broken, use `.filter` directly.
- `where(key, "is undefined")` does not match items whose key is explicitly `undefined`. Use `where(key, "not exists")`.

## Math

> **Auto-trigger:** code calls `c.sum`, `c.min`, `c.max`, `c.average`, `c.avg`, `c.median`, `c.plus`, `c.minus`, `c.multiply`, `c.divide`, `c.modulus`, `c.increment`, `c.decrement`, `c.double`, `c.half`, `c.even`, `c.odd`, `c.evenIndexes`, `c.oddIndexes`, `c.count`, `c.countValue`, or `c.countBy` on an `ImmutableCollection`; user asks "how do I sum / average / total / max / min on a collection field", "why does min return 0", "how to bump every item by 1", "why does divide throw".
> **Skip when:** math without a fluent chain or operator filter â€” use `mongez-reinforcements-arrays` (lighter `sum` / `min` / `max` / `average` / `median` / `count` / `countBy`); the higher-level "when to use which math method" tutorial â€” use `mongez-collection-math-aggregation`; aggregation downstream of `groupBy` â€” see `mongez-collection-transforming` or `mongez-collection-recipes`.

```ts
c.min(key?): number                              // 0 for empty array (reinforcements quirk)
c.max(key?): number                              // 0 for empty array
c.sum(key?): number                              // 0 for empty array
c.average(key?): number                          // NaN for empty array
c.avg(key?): number                              // alias for average
c.median(key?): number

c.plus(amount): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.plus(key, amount): ImmutableCollection<T>      // mutates source items via reinforcements `set`
c.minus(amount | key, amount): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.multiply(amount | key, amount): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.divide(amount | key, amount): ImmutableCollection<T>          // throws on 0 divisor
c.modulus(amount | key, amount): ImmutableCollection<T>         // throws on 0 divisor

c.increment(key?): ImmutableCollection<T>        // +1
c.decrement(key?): ImmutableCollection<T>        // -1
c.double(key?): ImmutableCollection<T>           // *2
c.half(key?): ImmutableCollection<T>             // /2

c.even(key?): ImmutableCollection<T>             // values whose key/item is even
c.odd(key?): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.evenIndexes(): ImmutableCollection<T>          // items at positions 0, 2, 4, ...
c.oddIndexes(): ImmutableCollection<T>           // items at positions 1, 3, 5, ...

c.count(keyOrCb): number
c.countValue(value): number
c.countBy(key): Record<string, number>
```

## Strings

> **Auto-trigger:** code calls `c.appendString`, `c.prependString`, `c.concatString`, `c.replaceString`, `c.replaceAllString`, `c.removeString`, `c.removeAllString`, `c.trim`, `c.string`, `c.number`, or `c.boolean` on an `ImmutableCollection`; user asks "how do I append / prepend / replace / strip / trim text on every item", "how to cast a collection of strings to numbers / booleans", "how to apply a string transform to one field of every object".
> **Skip when:** ad-hoc string mapping where `c.map(item => ...)` is clearer â€” use `mongez-collection-builtins`; single-string formatting helpers â€” those live in `@mongez/reinforcements` (string slugify / kebab / camel / template) and are out of scope here; `replaceAllString` with a `RegExp` value â€” it forces `new RegExp(s, "g")`, use `replaceString(/regex/g, ...)` instead.

```ts
// All keyed forms mutate the source items via reinforcements `set`.
c.appendString(s, key?): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.prependString(s, key?): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.concatString(s, key?): ImmutableCollection<T>           // identical to appendString for strings
c.replaceString(search: string | RegExp, repl: string, key?): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.replaceAllString(search: string, repl: string, key?): ImmutableCollection<T>  // search is always promoted to global regex
c.removeString(search: string | RegExp, key?): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.removeAllString(search: string, key?): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.trim(chars?: string, key?): ImmutableCollection<T>     // default chars = " "

// Casting
c.string(): ImmutableCollection<string>
c.number(): ImmutableCollection<number>
c.boolean(): ImmutableCollection<boolean>
```

## Pagination / slice / chunk

> **Auto-trigger:** code calls `c.take`, `c.limit`, `c.takeLast`, `c.takeUntil`, `c.takeWhile`, `c.skip`, `c.skipTo`, `c.skipLast`, `c.skipUntil`, `c.skipLastUntil`, `c.skipLastWhile`, `c.skipWhile`, `c.slice`, `c.splice`, `c.chunk`, `c.random`, or `c.shuffle` on an `ImmutableCollection`; user asks "how do I paginate a collection", "how to take the first N / last N / page N", "how to batch into chunks of 100", "how to grab a random sample / shuffle".
> **Skip when:** page metadata (total / hasNext / totalPages) â€” not built-in, manually compute from `.length`; chunk a plain array without a wrapper â€” use `chunk` from `mongez-reinforcements-arrays`; sorting before pagination â€” chain `sortBy` from `mongez-collection-sort-group` first.

```ts
c.take(n) / limit(n): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.takeLast(n): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.takeUntil(cb): ImmutableCollection<T>          // up to (exclusive) the first match
c.takeWhile(cb): ImmutableCollection<T>          // alias for filter â€” keeps every match

c.skip(n) / skipTo(n): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.skipLast(n): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.skipUntil(cb): ImmutableCollection<T>          // from (inclusive) the first match
c.skipLastUntil(cb): ImmutableCollection<T>      // keep up to (exclusive) the first match
c.skipLastWhile(cb): ImmutableCollection<T>      // drop a trailing run of matches
c.skipWhile(cb): ImmutableCollection<T>          // alias for reject

c.slice(start?, end?): ImmutableCollection<T>           // non-mutating
c.splice(start, deleteCount?, ...inserts): ImmutableCollection<T>  // non-mutating

c.chunk(size, returnAsCollection?: boolean = true): ImmutableCollection<ImmutableCollection<T> | T[]>

c.random(): T                                    // single random item
c.random(n): ImmutableCollection<T>              // n random items
c.shuffle(): ImmutableCollection<T>
```

## Sort / group / partition / unique

> **Auto-trigger:** code calls `c.sort`, `c.sortBy`, `c.sortByDesc`, `c.groupBy`, `c.partition`, `c.unique`, `c.uniqueList`, `c.swap`, `c.move`, `c.reorder`, `c.reverse`, or `c.flip` on an `ImmutableCollection`; user asks "how do I sort by a field / multiple keys", "ascending vs descending", "how to group by role / category", "how to split active vs inactive", "how to dedupe by email", "how to swap two positions / reorder a list".
> **Skip when:** mutation safety details for `sort` / `reverse` / `sortByDesc` â€” use `mongez-collection-mutation` for the in-depth matrix; column projection after grouping â€” see `mongez-collection-transforming` for `pluck` / `select`; standalone `groupBy` / `unique` without a wrapper â€” use `mongez-reinforcements-arrays` (lighter helpers); shuffle / random â€” see `mongez-collection-pagination`.

```ts
c.sort(cb?): ImmutableCollection<T>              // MUTATES â€” clone first
c.sortBy(key: string): ImmutableCollection<T>    // safe (clones)
c.sortBy({ [key]: "asc" | "desc" }): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.sortByDesc(key: string): ImmutableCollection<T>     // MUTATES

c.reverse() / flip(): ImmutableCollection<T>     // MUTATES

c.groupBy(key | keys): ImmutableCollection<{ [key]: any; items: T[] }>
c.partition(cb): [ImmutableCollection<T>, ImmutableCollection<T>]

c.unique(key?): ImmutableCollection                // values, when key given (not deduped objects)
c.uniqueList(key): ImmutableCollection<T>          // first object per unique key value
```

## Projection

> **Auto-trigger:** code calls `c.pluck`, `c.select`, `c.collectFrom`, `c.collectFromKey`, `c.partition`, `c.uniqueList` on an `ImmutableCollection`; user asks "how do I extract one field from every item", "how to project to a new shape / DTO", "how to keep only some keys per object", "how to flatten line items", "how to split into matching / not-matching"; chained pipelines that combine map + group + dedupe.
> **Skip when:** simple `map` / `filter` / `flat` / `flatMap` reference â€” use `mongez-collection-builtins`; operator filtering before transforming â€” chain via `mongez-collection-where` or `mongez-collection-querying`; the sort + group + unique reference matrix â€” use `mongez-collection-sort-group`; one-shot `pluck` / `groupBy` / `unique` without a chain â€” use `mongez-reinforcements-arrays` (lighter, tree-shakeable).

```ts
c.pluck(key | keys): ImmutableCollection
c.select(...keys): ImmutableCollection<Partial<T>>
c.collectFrom(key): ImmutableCollection            // flatten one level when key holds arrays
c.collectFromKey(index, key): ImmutableCollection
c.collectFromKey("index.key"): ImmutableCollection
```

## Clone

```ts
c.clone(): ImmutableCollection<T>
c.copy(): ImmutableCollection<T>                 // alias for clone
```

## Iteration

```ts
for (const item of c) { ... }
[...c]
Array.from(c)
```

## Why this package vs `@mongez/reinforcements`' array helpers

Both can do the same things; pick by ergonomics:

- One operation on an array â†’ reinforcements (lighter, tree-shakeable).
- Multi-step pipeline â†’ collection (fluent chain).
- `where(...)` operator filtering â†’ collection (no equivalent in reinforcements).

Collection delegates to reinforcements for many primitives (`min`, `max`, `sum`, `unique`, `pluck`, `groupBy`, `chunk`, `countBy`, `even`, `odd`, `shuffle`, `pushUnique`, `unshiftUnique`).

## What this package does NOT do

- React hooks / signals / atoms. Use [`@mongez/atom`](https://github.com/hassanzohdy/mongez-atom) â€” `atomCollection` is the equivalent for reactive arrays.
- Real immutability with structural sharing.
- Async iteration. All operations are synchronous and walk the array eagerly.
- Type predicates (use `@mongez/supportive-is`).
